content.full_text:
June 11, 1991


Marilyn Dorman
Phyllis Price-Meurer Election Committee
P.O. Box 1247
Salinas, CA 93902

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-91-253

Dear Ms. Dorman:

This is in response to your letter requesting confirmation of
telephone advice given pursuant to the Political Reform Act ("the
Act").1 Since your request was general in nature and did not
involve a specific transaction, we are treating your request as
one for informal assistance.2

On April 24, 1991 you and I had a telephone conversation in
which I indicated to you that campaign funds may be transferred
from the campaign account of one candidate to the campaign account
of another candidate. In order to meet the requirements of the
trust provision found in Section 89510, however, the transfer must
be reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose.

This advice supersedes previous Fair Political Practices
Commission advice in two respects. Under Section 85304,
candidates were forbidden to transfer their campaign funds to
other candidates. The court, in Service Employees International
Union, et al. v. Fair Political Practices Commission (E.D. Calif.,

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-91-253
Page 2


1990) 747 F.Supp. 590 ("SEIU"), struck down Section 85304 insofar
as it related to fiscal year schemes, and held candidates are
permitted to freely transfer their funds as a general rule.³

     Additionally, under the trust provision found in former
Section 85202, now Section 89510, we previously advised that a
candidate for office could expend campaign funds only for a
political purpose clearly related to his or her quest for that
particular office. (See Milman Advice Letter, I-90-206 and
Leidigh Advice Letter, I-90-492 (copies enclosed).) Because the
ruling in the SEIU case permitted candidates to transfer funds
freely, without reference to the particular office sought, we are
no longer interpreting the trust provision as previously done in
the Milman and Leidigh advice letters. As explained above, so
long as there is a reasonable relationship between the transfer of
campaign funds and a political purpose, the expenditure is
permitted.

     If you have further questions regarding the above, please do
not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrín
                                        Acting General Counsel
                                        
                                        [signature]
                                        By: Susan L. Borrow
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SH:SLB:ken

Enclosures


_____________________________

³     Please bear in mind that the prohibition on transfers set
forth in Section 85304 remains in effect in connection with
special elections. Additionally, you should determine whether or
not there are local restrictions regarding transfers of campaign
funds between candidate accounts.
